basics.name: Niklas Binder
basics.summary: PhD student / doctoral researcher in the Schürch Lab at University Hospital Tübingen, investigating the tumor microenvironment of brain tumors and other malignancies using spatial omics.

work.company: Institute of Pathology, University Hospital Tübingen (Schürch Lab)
work.summary: Investigating the tumor microenvironment of brain tumors and other malignancies using spatial omics approaches.

work.company: Institute of Neuropathology, University of Freiburg (Prinz Lab)
work.summary: Studied tumor-associated macrophages in the microenvironment of brain tumors using single-cell and spatial omics.
highlights: Single-cell RNA-seq, CITE-seq, and Multiome (RNA + ATAC)

publications.name: Primer on machine learning applications in brain immunology
publications.summary: A mini-review of machine learning applications in brain immunology, spanning conventional methods, deep learning, and emerging foundation models for single-cell and spatial data.

publications.name: CAR T cells, CAR NK cells, and CAR macrophages exhibit distinct traits in glioma models but are similarly enhanced when combined with cytokines
publications.summary: A systematic comparison of CAR T cells, CAR NK cells, and CAR macrophages in glioma models, showing distinct traits that are similarly enhanced by cytokine combination.

publications.name: EXTH-80. A systemic comparison of CAR T cells, CAR NK cells and CAR macrophages for glioma treatment
publications.summary: Conference abstract comparing CAR-based immune cell therapies for glioma treatment.
